title: updisocc
text: updisocc Discard one or more occurrences of the specified entity from the hitlist. Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> long updisocc (unsigned char*entity, longfrom_occ, longto_occ); Copy Parameters entity Mame of the entity. You can specify an empty string (but not zero) to denote the current entity. from_occ Sequence number of the first occurrence to be discarded. to_occ Sequence number of the last occurrence to be discarded. Return Values Table 1. Values Returned by updisocc Value Meaning >0 The sequence number of the occurrence that is current after discarding the occurrence. 0 No next occurrence is available. That is, the occurrence is the last occurrence or there is only one occurrence. -1 From_occ is greater than the number of available occurrences. -2 Entity does not exist or is not painted on the component. Use Library: yrtl Description updisocc removes one or more occurrences of entity from the component and the hitlist, without marking them for deletion. The argument entity is the name of the entity whose occurrences are to be discarded. If entity is an empty string, the current entity is used. Note: In contrast with the other occurrence-handling functions, you cannot specify a value of zero for entity . To denote the current entity, you must pass an empty string. updisocc is equivalent to the discard ProcScript statement, where you can find more information about the rules for discarding occurrences and the exact interpretation of the from_occ and to_occ parameters. Just like discard , this function alters the values of $hits , $curhits , $curocc , $dbocc , and $totdbocc The following C code uses updisocc in a loop to delete all occurrences, thereby simulating the clear/e ProcScript statement. Note that, in contrast with updelocc , updisocc does provide a different return code when the last occurrence has been processed. Otherwise this loop would not have been possible. Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> EXPORT void CLEAR_ENTITY () { long ret; for ( ;; ) { ret = updisocc("", 1, 1); if ( ret <= 0 ) break; } } Copy Because updisocc can delete a range of occurrences, one might be tempted to delete all occurrences in one statement instead of with the above loop, as shown below. However, the following does not work, even if the documentation might suggest that it should. Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> EXPORT void CLEAR_ENTITY () { updisocc("", 1, -1); /* This does NOT work !!! */ } Copy Related Links upaddocc Create an empty occurrence of an entity. updelocc Remove an occurrence of the specified entity from the hitlist. upsetocc Make the specified occurrence current.
